prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_development_entity formulation_dev_team_alpha)
(register_development_entity process_dev_team_alpha)
(register_development_entity product_program_alpha)
(register_development_entity product_program_beta)
(allocate_analytical_resource_to_entity formulation_dev_team_alpha analytical_resource_hplc_1)
(execute_assay_for_entity formulation_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(confirm_assay_result_for_entity formulation_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(formulation_team_validate_process_parameters formulation_dev_team_alpha process_parameter_set_standard_blend assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(release_assay_instance formulation_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(assign_formulation_variant_to_entity formulation_dev_team_alpha formulation_variant_a)
(formulation_team_record_experiment_result formulation_dev_team_alpha process_parameter_set_standard_blend formulation_variant_a)
(release_formulation_variant_from_entity formulation_dev_team_alpha formulation_variant_a)
(allocate_analytical_resource_to_entity process_dev_team_alpha analytical_resource_uv_vis_1)
(execute_assay_for_entity process_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(confirm_assay_result_for_entity process_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(process_team_validate_manufacturing_parameters process_dev_team_alpha manufacturing_parameter_set_batch_scale assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(release_assay_instance process_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(assign_formulation_variant_to_entity process_dev_team_alpha formulation_variant_a)
(process_team_record_experiment_result process_dev_team_alpha manufacturing_parameter_set_batch_scale formulation_variant_a)
(release_formulation_variant_from_entity process_dev_team_alpha formulation_variant_a)
(assemble_development_option formulation_dev_team_alpha process_dev_team_alpha process_parameter_set_standard_blend manufacturing_parameter_set_batch_scale development_option_candidate_a)
(execute_assay_for_entity formulation_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(lab_validate_development_option development_option_candidate_a formulation_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(signoff_formulation_team_readiness formulation_dev_team_alpha development_option_candidate_a)
(signoff_process_team_readiness process_dev_team_alpha development_option_candidate_a)
(schedule_stability_study_for_entity formulation_dev_team_alpha stability_study_accelerated assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(finalize_team_scaleup_readiness formulation_dev_team_alpha analytical_resource_hplc_1 stability_study_accelerated)
(release_assay_instance formulation_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(execute_assay_for_entity process_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(schedule_stability_study_for_entity process_dev_team_alpha stability_study_accelerated assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(finalize_process_team_scaleup_readiness process_dev_team_alpha analytical_resource_uv_vis_1 stability_study_accelerated)
(release_assay_instance process_dev_team_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(allocate_analytical_resource_to_entity product_program_alpha analytical_resource_hplc_1)
(execute_assay_for_entity product_program_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(confirm_assay_result_for_entity product_program_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(validate_analytical_package_for_program product_program_alpha analytical_package_core development_option_candidate_a)
(approve_analytical_package product_program_alpha analytical_package_core development_option_candidate_a assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(assign_equipment_spec_to_program product_program_alpha equipment_specification_homogenizer)
(assign_formulation_variant_to_entity product_program_alpha formulation_variant_a)
(run_equipment_validation product_program_alpha equipment_specification_homogenizer formulation_variant_a analytical_package_core development_option_candidate_a)
(assign_regulatory_interaction_to_program product_program_alpha regulatory_interaction_pre_submission_meeting)
(finalize_regulatory_interaction_for_program product_program_alpha regulatory_interaction_pre_submission_meeting analytical_package_core development_option_candidate_a)
(record_program_signoff product_program_alpha)
(schedule_stability_study_for_entity product_program_alpha stability_study_accelerated assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(finalize_program_scaleup_readiness product_program_alpha analytical_resource_hplc_1 stability_study_accelerated)
(release_formulation_variant_from_entity product_program_alpha formulation_variant_a)
(release_equipment_specification_from_program product_program_alpha equipment_specification_homogenizer)
(release_regulatory_interaction_from_program product_program_alpha regulatory_interaction_pre_submission_meeting)
(release_assay_instance product_program_alpha assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(allocate_analytical_resource_to_entity product_program_beta analytical_resource_uv_vis_1)
(execute_assay_for_entity product_program_beta assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(confirm_assay_result_for_entity product_program_beta assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(validate_analytical_package_for_program product_program_beta analytical_package_extended development_option_candidate_a)
(approve_analytical_package product_program_beta analytical_package_extended development_option_candidate_a assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(assign_equipment_spec_to_program product_program_beta equipment_specification_homogenizer)
(assign_formulation_variant_to_entity product_program_beta formulation_variant_a)
(run_equipment_validation product_program_beta equipment_specification_homogenizer formulation_variant_a analytical_package_extended development_option_candidate_a)
(assign_regulatory_interaction_to_program product_program_beta regulatory_interaction_pre_submission_meeting)
(finalize_regulatory_interaction_for_program product_program_beta regulatory_interaction_pre_submission_meeting analytical_package_extended development_option_candidate_a)
(record_program_signoff product_program_beta)
(schedule_stability_study_for_entity product_program_beta stability_study_accelerated assay_instance_dissolution_1)
(finalize_program_scaleup_readiness product_program_beta analytical_resource_uv_vis_1 stability_study_accelerated)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a pharmaceutics release profile design tradeoff domain. The goal is to make four entities ready for scale-up: formulation_dev_team_alpha, process_dev_team_alpha, product_program_alpha, and product_program_beta.


## Examining scale-up readiness  
- The task centers on preparing four entities for scale-up in pharmaceutics: formulation_dev_team_alpha, process_dev_team_alpha, product_program_alpha, and product_program_beta.  
- The focus is on designing release profiles, balancing tradeoffs to ensure all components are ready for larger production.

## Examining scale-up readiness  
- Both product_program_alpha and product_program_beta share formulation_dev_team_alpha and process_dev_team_alpha, along with development_option_candidate_a.  
- Analytical packages differ: alpha uses core, beta uses extended, while other resources like assay_instance (dissolution_1) and materials (excipient_lactose_monohydrate) are singular.  

## Checking final signoff requirements  
- Final signoff for teams requires signoff_formulation_team_readiness and signoff_process_team_readiness, needing development_option assembled and lab_verified, but not final_signoff yet.  
- For programs, final signoff involves record_program_signoff, final review, and partner integration, all essential for ready_for_scale_up, which also needs stability_scheduled.  

## Exploring development option assembly  
- Assembling development_option_candidate_a requires teams ready, experiments completed, and parameters validated, with variants like with_formulation_validation or with_full_validations.  
- With_full_validations needs process and manufacturing parameters validated with material, but doesn’t require team experiments completed.  

## Reviewing initial setup  
- All entities (formulation and process teams, both programs) need register_development_entity since initial state has no entity_registered.  
- Available resources include three analytical tools (hplc_1, uv_vis_1, ms_1), one stability option, and two regulatory inputs, with external partners and supply constraints also in play.

## Reviewing resource constraints  
- There are only three analytical resources (hplc_1, uv_vis_1, ms_1) available for four entities, creating a bottleneck for validation and readiness.  
- Analytical resources are released only during finalize_..._scaleup_readiness actions, with no other release mechanisms identified.  

## Exploring resource allocation strategies  
- Consider sequencing finalization: finalize one entity to release its resource, then allocate it to the next entity needing validation.  
- This approach relies on ensuring stability_scheduled and final_signoff, which require team readiness and experiment completion beforehand.  

## Checking validation necessities  
- All development entities (teams and programs) need entity_validated for most actions, including experiments and final signoff, which require analytical resources.  
- No initial assay results exist, and no alternative paths to validation were found, highlighting the resource shortage issue.

## Exploring resource allocation strategies  
- All entities require validation through confirm_assay_result, creating a dependency on limited analytical resources.  
- Finalizing scaleup for one entity releases its resource, allowing reallocation to validate the next entity, suggesting a sequential process.  

## Planning entity registration  
- Register both formulation and process teams first, as they share resources like formulation_variant and material.  
- Allocate analytical resource (e.g., hplc_1) to formulation team to start the validation process, followed by executing and confirming assays.  

## Managing validation steps  
- After confirming assay results, use them to validate process parameters, ensuring the team is ready for experiments without material validation.  
- Assign formulation variant to the team and record experiment results to mark the team as ready and experiment completed, leveraging existing assay results.

## Managing validation steps
- After validating process parameters, assign a formulation variant to the team for experiments without needing additional assay results.  
- Record experiment results to mark the team as ready and experiment completed, using existing assay results for both formulation and process teams.  

## Planning team readiness
- Lab validation requires an assembled development option, a ready formulation team, and existing assay results, confirming readiness for signoff.  
- Both formulation and process teams can achieve final signoff after lab verification, ensuring all prerequisites like team readiness and experiment completion are met.  

## Scheduling stability studies
- Schedule stability studies for each team using available assay results, noting only one stability resource is available, requiring sequential allocation.  
- Finalizing scaleup readiness for one team releases both the analytical resource and stability, allowing allocation to the next team.  

## Finalizing scaleup readiness
- Finalizing scaleup for the formulation team first with hplc_1 and stability_study_accelerated frees resources for the process team.  
- Process team then uses uv_vis_1 and the same stability study, ensuring both teams achieve ready_for_scale_up status.

## Managing assay availability  
- Assay results are tied to specific entity-assay pairs, allowing multiple entities to use results independently.  
- Execute an assay for one entity consumes availability, uses the result, then releases it for the next entity, ensuring sequential processing.  

## Coordinating team validation  
- Validation for each team requires executing assays sequentially, releasing resources after all result-dependent steps like parameter validation and scheduling.  
- Teams must complete experiments and record results before assembling, requiring careful sequencing to manage shared resources like formulation variants.  

## Adjusting validation sequence  
- Parameter validation and lab validation for one team must occur before moving to the next, ensuring assay results are available when needed.  
- Interleaving assay execution and release helps manage limited resources, allowing both teams to progress without conflicts.

## Evaluating experiment completion

- Experiment completion (exp_completed) can be set using complete_iteration, which requires validated_with_material, material assigned, and assay results.
- Complete_iteration also sets param_validated and releases material, allowing sequential processing for both teams.

## Managing material allocation

- Material is shared and limited to one, requiring teams to test sequentially: assign to one team, validate, then release for the next.
- Test_material sets validated_with_material and ready status, but assembling both teams simultaneously is blocked until material is available for all.

## Evaluating assay execution

- Assays can be re-executed for a team even if a result already exists, consuming availability and setting a new result.  
- Re-executing allows restoring assay results needed for later steps like lab validation, without requiring prior result absence.  

## Managing assay release and re-execution

- Release assays after parameter validation to free resources, then re-execute later for steps like lab validation and scheduling.  
- This approach ensures both teams can progress sequentially, managing shared resources like formulation variants and assays.  

## Handling team signoff requirements

- Final signoff for teams requires readiness, experiment completion, assembly, and lab verification, but not ongoing assay results.  
- Process team signoff doesn’t need assay results after release, while formulation team may need re-execution for scheduling.  

## Coordinating scaleup readiness

- After final signoff, schedule and finalize scaleup for the formulation team, using re-executed assay results and stability studies.  
- Process team may need additional assay re-execution for scheduling, ensuring all resources are available for scaleup readiness.

## Examining variant and program validation

- Variants like variant_a may meet process but not formulation criteria, affecting readiness for analytical interpretation (ready_for_anal_int).
- Variant_c meets both, while standard assembly (assemble_development_option) does not set meets_formulation_validation or meets_process_validation, requiring separate actions.

## Assessing analytical package integration

- Approve_analytical_package sets equipment_spec_confirmed_for_program, requiring a validated program, associated option, and assay results, but not meets_formulation.
- Integrate_analytical_package_with_program also sets equipment confirmation, needing meets_formulation, regulatory input linked, and additional conditions like assay results.

## Exploring equipment validation requirements

- Running equipment validation requires equipment confirmation, equipment specs, and formulation assignment, but not meets_process_validation, setting has_validated_equipment.
- Performing equipment validation follows a similar path but requires meets_process_validation, ensuring equipment is validated for regulatory completeness.

## Reviewing regulatory and quality completion

- Quality completion (qual_complete) and regulatory steps like finalize_regul_int require program_has_validated_equipment, with no bypass paths identified.
- Record_signoff needs qual_complete but blocks ready_for_anal_int, indicating sequential dependencies in the validation process.

## Tracking assay execution and release

- For alpha, assay results were used for approval but not needed for equipment validation, allowing release after finalization.
- Shared resources like equipment specs and formulation variants were released post-alpha, making them available for beta.

## Managing beta program setup

- Beta program registered, allocated UV-Vis resource, and executed dissolution assay, confirming results for validation.
- Analytical package for beta was validated, associated with the option, and approved, setting equipment confirmation.

## Validating equipment for beta

- Equipment spec and formulation variant assigned to beta, followed by equipment validation, ensuring has_validated_equipment.
- Regulatory interaction assigned and finalized, setting quality completion for beta, leading to program signoff.

## Finalizing scaleup for beta

- Stability study scheduled for beta using available assay results, meeting scaleup readiness requirements.
- Finalized program scaleup for beta, confirming all conditions for readiness, with unused resources like MS_1 noted.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
